name: Fried Chicken Breasts Rubbed in Ancho Peppers
recipeIngredient: 6 Ancho (or mulato) peppers, stemmed and seeded
recipeInstructions:
1. Rinse and pat dry 2 boneless, skinless chicken breasts. Separate into 8 pieces (breasts and tenderloins).
2. Pound the chicken flat to about a 50% increase in size.
3. Score the meat in a crisscross pattern with a sharp knife.
4. Toast dried peppers on a hot iron skillet until fragrant. Remove stems and seeds, then place in a pan. Cover with water, bring to a boil, remove from heat, and steep for 1 hour.
5. Blend or puree all rub ingredients into a smooth paste using a blender or food mill. Allow to cool.
6. Mix seasoned coating ingredients thoroughly.
7. Dust chicken pieces on all sides with flour, allowing to dry for 5 minutes.
8. Spread ancho paste evenly on one side of the chicken pieces, rubbing it into the scored pattern. The paste should cover and "hide" the exposed meat.
9. Sprinkle seasoned coating liberally over the pasted chicken, patting it in firmly. Allow to dry for 5 minutes.
10. Heat olive oil in a hot iron skillet. Add butter and reduce heat to medium. Cook chicken breasts first (followed by tenderloins) for 5 minutes, then flip and cook another 5 minutes.
11. Remove meat from the pan and place on a warming rack in a 300°F oven. Continue cooking remaining pieces.
12. Serve with yellow corn and black bean salsa made with onions, balsamic vinegar, and chicken broth.
